nodes.context: Italian criminal society originating in Sicily, dating back to mid-19th century, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, etc.
Offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia created in the United States due to emigration from Sicily.

A coalition of mafia clans led by Luciano Leggio and later Salvatore Riina, which dominated the Sicilian Mafia during the Second Mafia War.
Calabrian crime organization that took over most of the illegal drug trade from Cosa Nostra by the late 1990s.
Neapolitan crime organization; mentioned as an ally of the Renzvillo crime family.

A mafia family that set up alliances with 'Ndrangheta and Camorra, involved in drug trade and extortion.
New York crime family with links to Cosa Nostra.

Mafia group in Corleone, Sicily, active in the 1890s.

edges.evidence_quote: By the 20th century, wide-scale emigration from Sicily led to the formation of mafiosi-style gangs in other countries, in particular in the United States, where its offshoot, the American Mafia, was created.
edges.context: The American Mafia is an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia, created by Sicilian emigrants.

edges.evidence_quote: By the 20th century, wide-scale emigration from Sicily led to the formation of mafiosi-style gangs in other countries, in particular in the United States, where its offshoot, the American Mafia, was created.
edges.context: The American Mafia is an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia, created by Sicilian emigrants.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1962, mafia boss Cesare Manzella organized a drug shipment to the United States with the help of two Sicilian clans, the Grecos and the La Barberas.
edges.context: The Grecos cooperated with the Sicilian Mafia in a drug shipment.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1962, mafia boss Cesare Manzella organized a drug shipment to the United States with the help of two Sicilian clans, the Grecos and the La Barberas.
edges.context: The La Barberas cooperated with the Sicilian Mafia in a drug shipment.

edges.evidence_quote: The La Barberas murdered Di Pisa and Manzella, triggering a war.
edges.context: The La Barberas went to war with the Grecos after a dispute over a drug shipment.

edges.evidence_quote: By the 20th century, wide-scale emigration from Sicily led to the formation of mafiosi-style gangs in other countries, in particular in the United States, where its offshoot, the American Mafia, was created.

edges.evidence_quote: He initiated a campaign to dominate Cosa Nostra and its narcotics trade.
edges.context: The Corleonesi waged a campaign to dominate the Sicilian Mafia (Cosa Nostra).

edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1990s, the weakened Cosa Nostra had to yield most of the illegal drug trade to the 'Ndrangheta crime organization from Calabria.
edges.context: Cosa Nostra yielded the illegal drug trade to 'Ndrangheta.

edges.evidence_quote: The Renzvillo mafia family has allegedly set up alliances with the 'Ndrangheta and Camorra.
edges.context: The Renzvillo crime family formed alliances with 'Ndrangheta.

edges.context: The Renzvillo crime family formed alliances with Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: News articles also confirmed links between the Cosa Nostra and New York's Gambino crime family.
edges.context: Cosa Nostra has links with the Gambino crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: By the 20th century, wide-scale emigration from Sicily led to the formation of mafiosi-style gangs in other countries, in particular in the United States, where its offshoot, the American Mafia, was created.
edges.context: The American Mafia is an offshoot of the Sicilian Mafia, formed by Sicilian emigrants.

edges.evidence_quote: In order to give the movement teeth and to protect himself from harm, Verro became a member of a Mafia group in Corleone, the Fratuzzi (Little Brothers).
edges.context: The Fratuzzi is a Mafia group in Corleone, part of the Sicilian Mafia.
